H1.note: Extremely large effect size (d > 3). Adequately powered even with n=20 models.

H2.note: Ceiling effect: cosine = 0.977 with std = 0.014. Power is effectively 1.0. The real constraint is the upper bound on cosine, not statistical power.

H5.hypothesis: H5: Reversed diagonal (national models collapse more)
H5.test: one-sample t-test on per-pair advantage scores
H5.direction: reversed (national models collapse MORE, opposite to prediction)
H5.interpretation: n=8 pairs is low. Null result could reflect insufficient power.

H6.hypothesis: H6: Western DCI < non-Western DCI (training breadth effect)
H6.note: Large n (>460 total calls) ensures high power even for small effects. d ~ 0.3 with n1=148, n2=314 gives power > 0.90.

interpretation: H1 and H6: extremely high power; null findings would be reliable.
H2: ceiling effect on cosine; power is effectively 1.0.
H5: low n=8 pairs limits power for small effects, but the reversal is statistically significant (p<0.05), so the finding is a real reversal, not a power failure.
